description: Baseline trainable board CNN smoke run, 20k rows 16 channels 3 epochs

asi.hypothesis: A real board-input CNN should beat frozen random feature heads once stabilized; this smoke establishes baseline.
asi.learned: Trainer runs successfully on CUDA, loss decreases, but 3-epoch 20k/16ch top8=0.06975 is below frozen MLP top8=0.10205; need more epochs/channels or better architecture.

description: Increase board CNN training to 8 epochs on 20k rows

asi.hypothesis: The CNN was undertrained in the smoke baseline; more epochs should close the gap to frozen MLP.
asi.learned: Top8 improved from 0.06975 to 0.0955 and CE dropped strongly; still just under frozen MLP top8=0.10205, so more training/data/capacity may beat it.

description: Increase board CNN width to 32 channels at 8 epochs

asi.hypothesis: More trainable conv channels should increase capacity and beat 16-channel CNN.
asi.learned: 32 channels improves top8 only modestly to 0.10025, nearly frozen MLP but not clearly beyond it; loss/CE improved, suggesting capacity helps but heldout top-k is slow.

description: Scale board CNN to 50k rows with 32 channels and 8 epochs

asi.hypothesis: More supervised positions should improve heldout top-k and move diversity for the trainable CNN.
asi.learned: This is the first clear win over frozen MLP: top8=0.1308 vs frozen MLP 0.10205, top4=0.0796, top1=0.0264. Trainable board representation is validated.

description: Export board CNN weights and train 50k 32-channel model for 10 epochs

asi.hypothesis: Two extra epochs plus weight export should preserve the 50k CNN gain and produce an artifact usable for FE inference wiring.
asi.learned: Top8 nudged from 0.1308 to 0.1315, top1 improved to 0.0302, and artifact now includes conv/head weights; next step is implement evaluator support or scale rows/epochs.

description: Scale trainable board CNN to 100k rows for 8 epochs

asi.hypothesis: Increasing row count should continue improving policy recall now that trainable CNN has beaten frozen baselines.
asi.learned: 100k improves top8 to 0.1358 and top4 to 0.0826, but dev CE worsens versus 50k/10ep and top1 drops; scaling helps top-k modestly but likely needs better architecture or LR schedule.

description: Try wider 64-channel CNN on 50k rows for 8 epochs

asi.hypothesis: A wider trainable CNN should improve top-k by increasing board representation capacity.
asi.rollback_reason: Top8=0.1328 is worse than current best 0.1358 from 100k rows/32 channels; width alone does not beat data scaling.
asi.next_action_hint: Try 100k rows with 10-12 epochs or add depth/residual blocks before increasing width further.

description: Train 100k-row 32-channel board CNN longer for 12 epochs

asi.hypothesis: The 100k/32ch CNN was still undertrained; extending from 8 to 12 epochs should improve heldout top-k without changing data or architecture.
asi.learned: Clear improvement: top8 0.1358 -> 0.1492, top4 0.0826 -> 0.0932, top1 0.0278 -> 0.0312, CE improved to 6.0427. Longer training is currently the most promising path; loss was still decreasing through epoch 12.

description: Extend 100k-row 32-channel board CNN training to 16 epochs

asi.hypothesis: Since 100k/32ch loss and top-k were still improving at 12 epochs, extending to 16 epochs should continue improving without changing benchmark data or architecture.
asi.learned: Improved again: top8 0.1492 -> 0.1570, top4 0.0932 -> 0.1007, top1 0.0312 -> 0.0392, CE 6.0427 -> 6.0192. Loss still decreased through epoch 16, but marginal top8 gain is smaller than 8->12; next compare 20 epochs or LR schedule/data scaling.
